@graph.headline: agent-rollback: The Undo Button for OpenAI Codex CLI
@graph.description: A CLI, MCP server, and Codex hook that captures content-addressed snapshots of your workspace so you can undo, revert, rollback, and checkpoint any Codex change in one command.
@graph.keywords: codex undo, codex revert, codex checkpoint, codex rollback, agent snapshot layer, codex mcp

mainEntity.name: How do I undo a Codex change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Run `agent-rollback list` (or short alias `arb list`), find the checkpoint taken before the bad edit, then `agent-rollback revert cp-<id> --yes`. With Codex hooks installed (`agent-rollback init codex`), Codex auto-snapshots before every prompt and tool use, so there's always a checkpoint to roll back to.

mainEntity.name: How do I revert just one bad Codex operation, not the whole workspace?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the operation log: `agent-rollback log` lists recent operations, and `agent-rollback op revert op-<id> --yes` reverts exactly that operation and leaves later unrelated edits alone. A safety checkpoint is created first, so it's always undoable.

mainEntity.name: How do I undo the last Codex edit (or the last N edits)?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use `agent-rollback undo N --yes` to undo the last N checkpoints. Each undo creates a safety checkpoint automatically so the undo itself is undoable.

mainEntity.name: How do I diff what Codex changed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use `agent-rollback diff cp-before cp-after` for changed paths plus a summary, or `agent-rollback diff cp-before cp-after --patch` for a full unified diff. From a Codex chat, just say 'what changed between the auth checkpoint and now?'.

mainEntity.name: How do I add a safety net so every Codex run is auto-rolled-back-able?

acceptedAnswer.text: Install Codex hooks once per repo with `agent-rollback init codex`. Run `/hooks` inside Codex to trust the generated repo-local hooks. From then on, every session start, user prompt, before-tool-use, and after-tool-use event creates a deduped auto-checkpoint.

mainEntity.name: Does it work with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Cline, or Gemini CLI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. The CLI works anywhere. Install the agent skill globally for 18+ agents with `npx skills add Nainish-Rai/agent-rollback --skill agent-rollback -g -y`. The MCP server is registered automatically for Codex CLI on install with `--with-mcp`.

mainEntity.name: How is this different from git stash, git restore, or git reset?

acceptedAnswer.text: agent-rollback works in any directory (not just Git repos), auto-snapshots on every Codex prompt and tool use, supports operation-level undo via an append-only ops log, and exposes an MCP server + SKILL.md so any AI agent can call it. It is the agent-native complement to Git, not a replacement.
No. Everything lives in `.agent-rollback/` inside your project (or wherever `--store` points). There is no telemetry, no cloud sync, no analytics. The CLI and MCP server are 100% local.

@graph.name: How to undo a Codex change with agent-rollback
@graph.description: Install agent-rollback, initialize a repo, create a checkpoint, then restore it when Codex goes off track.

step.name: Install
step.text: Run `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Nainish-Rai/agent-rollback/main/scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --all` (or `npm install -g agent-rollback` for the binary only).

step.name: Initialize
step.text: Inside your repo, run `agent-rollback init` (or `arb init`). This creates `.agent-rollback/` in the project.

step.name: Checkpoint + do something risky
step.text: From a Codex chat: 'make a checkpoint called green tests and refactor the auth module'. Or from the CLI: `agent-rollback checkpoint 'green tests'` then `agent-rollback run codex 'refactor the auth module'`.

step.name: Browse checkpoints
step.text: From Codex: 'show me checkpoints'. From the CLI: `agent-rollback list` or `agent-rollback tui` for the interactive browser.

step.name: Roll back when something goes wrong
step.text: From Codex: 'go to the last checkpoint'. From the CLI: `agent-rollback revert cp-<id> --yes` or `agent-rollback undo 1 --yes`. A safety checkpoint is created automatically.

step.name: See what changed
step.text: From Codex: 'what did the refactor change vs the green tests checkpoint?'. From the CLI: `agent-rollback diff cp-before cp-after` or `agent-rollback diff cp-before cp-after --patch`.

step.name: Optional: add a permanent safety net
step.text: Run `agent-rollback init codex` once per repo, then `/hooks` inside Codex to trust the auto-snapshot hooks. Every prompt and tool use now creates a deduped auto-checkpoint.
